capabilities: Implement chain-of-thought suppression by integrating domain-specific constraints and ensuring error-free execution with comprehensive audit logging.

procedure.action: Compose domain-specific constraints by using a meta.protocol derived knowledge base and apply suppression logic through a custom middleware layer; ensure middleware logs all suppression events with timestamps by composing module interfaces via adapter pattern; assert input/output type contracts match before connecting; assert integration_error_count < 1 after contract validation.
Execute the suppression logic by invoking the chain_of_thought_suppress function with domain-specific parameters; assert that error_count < 1 and result_valid = true post-execution.

Log operation_duration_ms and result_code to an audit trail by appending entries to a centralized logging system using a structured logging format like JSON using domain-appropriate tooling; assert error_count < 1 and result_valid = true; log operation_duration_ms and result_code to audit trail.

Restate the suppression procedure with domain-specific examples by documenting each step in a knowledge base entry; include example inputs and expected outputs using domain-appropriate tooling; assert error_count < 1 and result_valid = true; log operation_duration_ms and result_code to audit trail.
Verify the suppression logic by running a test suite with domain-specific scenarios; ensure all tests pass with error_count < 1 and result_valid = true.

summary: Implement chain-of-thought suppression in derived knowledge bases by applying domain-specific constraints and ensuring error-free execution with audit logging.
title: Chain-of-Thought Suppression

failureConditions: Suppression fails when domain-specific constraints are not integrated, causing incorrect reasoning paths to persist in the knowledge base.
Audit trail becomes unreliable when operation_duration_ms and result_code are not logged, causing difficulty in tracing suppression events.
metrics: input/output type contracts match before connecting
that error_count < 1 and result_valid = true post-execution
